danger_signals.visual_form: Figures wearing sunglasses, ski masks, or gas masks
danger_signals.meaning: Armed threats to be neutralized; obscured faces indicate enemy combatants

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy with exposed face in civilian-free environment
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring elimination; face exposure signals heightened danger level

danger_signals.visual_form: Player reverts to basic revolver after being shot while holding upgraded weapon
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; player has sustained damage and lost tactical advantage

danger_signals.visual_form: Barefaced figures without masks in same environment as armed enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Collateral damage risk; shooting these figures triggers rank penalty

safety_signals.meaning: Friendly forces; must not be harmed to maintain rank progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Barefaced non-combatant figures in public spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Protected non-targets; shooting causes rank demotion or stasis

safety_signals.visual_form: In-stage weapon pickups (Magnum, shotgun, assault rifle, SMG, grenade launcher)
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage; temporary enhanced firepower until player sustains damage

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban police environments (bank, downtown areas, chemical plant)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Masked/goggled appearance (sunglasses, ski masks, gas masks)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varying mask types indicate different enemy unit types or stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bank robbery locations, Chinatown/Downtown assault zones, hijacking sites, drug dealer operations, chemical plant sabotage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Armed masked enemies proliferating across urban environments; civilians present in combat zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively engage with weapons; dispatcher call initiates emergency protocol

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player is one of two surviving elite officers; rest hospitalized or dead
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single or two-player cooperative gameplay; reduced police presence in environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage progression from bank robbery → hijacking → drug operations → chemical plant sabotage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters intensify; weapon variety increases; civilian-criminal distinction becomes clearer

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rank display downgrades when civilians are shot
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mechanical penalty for rule violations; rank floor at Patrolman prevents infinite demotion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Promotion from Patrolman → Detective → Sergeant → Lieutenant → Captain → Commander
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Post-stage rank increase tied to zero civilian casualties

environmental_storytelling.example: Stages named 'The Bank Robbery', 'Chinatown Assault', 'The Hijacking', 'Drug Dealers', 'Chemical Plant Sabotage' tell story of escalating crime organization activity across Chicago infrastructure
Entirely digitized photographs and sprites create immersive urban crime scenario; realistic civilian and officer appearances enhance identification stakes
Civilians appear in bank, downtown, and other public spaces to establish normal city life disrupted by criminal activity
Boss fights occur in civilian-free zones, signaling shift from collateral-risk to direct confrontation mode
Progression from basic .38 revolver to assault rifles, submachine guns, and grenade launchers reflects escalating threat level and tactical response needs

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Emerge from environments with masks/goggles; engage player with weapons; one-shot death indicates vulnerability but threatening nature
Face exposed (barefaced unlike regular enemies); fought in isolated civilian-free arena; requires tactical elimination to progress stage
Barefaced in environment; must not be shot; presence indicates civilian-friendly zones requiring discrimination
Barefaced and unarmed; present in public spaces; shooting triggers rank penalty; presence forces player restraint discipline
Maintains current weapon tier until sustaining damage; reverts to revolver on hit; rank advances/degrades based on collateral damage

notes: Lethal Enforcers uses photorealistic digitization to create morally-complex visual communication. The core danger/safety signaling mechanism relies entirely on facial obscuration (masks = threat, barefaced = ally or protected civilian). The rank system mechanically reinforces visual discrimination through consequences—players must learn to read the environment to maintain rank progression. World state manifests through geographic escalation (bank → plant sabotage) and NPC distribution patterns. The game's controversial photorealism amplifies the visual distinction between combatants and non-combatants, making the moral choice to avoid civilian casualties visually explicit.
